journal_entry.description: Issue common stock for cash ($100 par value, $80 additional paid-in capital)
journal_entry.rationale: Issuing common stock increases cash (asset) and increases equity through two components: Common Stock at par value ($100) and Additional Paid-In Capital for the excess over par ($80). This follows IFRS equity accounting standards where stock is recorded at par with any premium recorded separately. No tax applies to equity transactions.

output.reason: Issuing common stock creates two equity increases: (1) Common Stock at par value $100 and (2) Additional Paid-In Capital (APIC) $80. Both are equity accounts being credited, resulting in 2 equity increase lines.

credit_classifier.reason: Issuing common stock creates two equity increases: (1) Common Stock at par value $100 and (2) Additional Paid-In Capital (APIC) $80. Both are equity accounts being credited, resulting in 2 equity increase lines.

output.reason: Issuing common stock for cash: the debit side records cash received = asset increase. The $180 cash inflow is one debit line increasing the asset account (Cash).

debit_classifier.reason: Issuing common stock for cash: the debit side records cash received = asset increase. The $180 cash inflow is one debit line increasing the asset account (Cash).
